description_long: This machine gun features a compact, angular body with a matte dark finish, likely constructed from metal and polymer components. The stock is a minimalist, skeletonized design, providing structural support while reducing weight, and is attached to the rear of the receiver. The magazine is a detachable box type, inserted at an angle into the lower receiver, and appears robust for rapid reloading. The barrel is short and cylindrical, with no visible fluting or heavy cooling features, and is integrated directly into the main body. The upper receiver includes a simple, raised sight post and a rear notch, forming a basic iron sight configuration. The overall design is utilitarian, with clean lines and minimal external attachments, suitable for urban tactical scenarios.
description: A compact, angular machine gun with a skeletonized stock, box magazine, and simple iron sights, finished in matte dark tones. Its design is utilitarian and streamlined for urban tactical use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, including the skeletonized stock, angled box magazine, and the short, cylindrical barrel.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow barrel profile and the alignment of the iron sights, with minimal width visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the main features of the first side, with clear visibility of the stock and magazine.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the stock's open framework and the rear sight, with the magazine and barrel mostly obscured.
